name: Apple Gingerbread (Scottish)
recipeInstructions:
1. Set the oven to 350°F (175°C). Grease an 8-inch x 6-inch baking tin.
2. Peel, core, and slice the apples.
3. Put the apple slices into a pan with a little water and sufficient sugar to taste. Stew gently until tender, then mash and cool.
4. In a separate pan, melt the golden syrup and butter together gently until dissolved. Add the demerara sugar and let it cool.
5. Sift the all-purpose flour, ground ginger, and ground cloves into a bowl and make a well in the center.
6. Add the egg to the syrup mixture and beat well into the flour. Stir in the mashed apples and continue to beat until everything is well combined.
7. Turn the batter into the prepared tin and cook for 30 minutes until firm.
8. Let it stand for a few minutes until firm before turning out onto a wire rack to cool. Cut into slices.
